description: Nested COALESCE where the outer call has a literal default is non-nullable

columns.name: val
columns.note: outer COALESCE has literal default, guaranteeing non-NULL even though inner COALESCE is nullable

name: nested_coalesce
query_sql:
-- @name GetNestedCoalesce
-- @returns :many
SELECT COALESCE(COALESCE(u.bio, o.notes), 'default') AS val FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe recursively analyzes nested COALESCE expressions to determine nullability
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc does not analyze nested COALESCE and marks the result as nullable
